evals.prompt: Build me a single-file HTML hotsite called 'threat-intel.w-b.dev' that explains a cybersecurity threat intelligence pipeline. It should have 3 content sections: one explaining data ingestion, one covering normalization/enrichment, and one on detection rules. Use a flow chain component in the first section and cards in the second.
evals.expected_output: A complete, single-file index.html that uses the w-b.dev design system tokens exactly: dark background (#0e0e0e), warm paper foreground (#f3f1ec), JetBrains Mono + Helvetica fonts, amber accent (#d4a853), scroll-driven IntersectionObserver reveal, section labels in '01 // Name' format, no border-radius on cards, author attribution link at the end.

evals.prompt: I need a new section for my w-b.dev page that compares our old alert triage approach to the new one. Build a split-panel comparison component showing 'Before' on the left (old, dimmed) and 'After' on the right (accent-bordered). Include 4 bullet points on each side using the monospace style.
evals.expected_output: HTML+CSS for a content section that uses the split-panel component pattern: .split > .split-panel.old + .split-panel.new, old panel at opacity 0.4, new panel with accent-dim border, monospace labels, no border-radius, uses design tokens for all colors.

evals.prompt:
I have this page snippet and want you to review it and fix any design system violations: ```html

Pipeline Overview

Our data pipeline processes 10M events/day.

Active — Processing
```
evals.expected_output: Fixed HTML that applies design system tokens: --bg (#0e0e0e) background, --fg (#f3f1ec) for headline, --dim for body, --accent for the active state, removes border-radius and box-shadow, uses Helvetica/JetBrains Mono, and explains each violation fixed.
